codeReview.note: Browserbase team maintains; PRs go through Browserbase internal review.

secretsHandling.note: API key + project ID via env. Browserbase's keys are per-project, scoped.

sandboxModel.note: Browser runs in Browserbase's sandbox infrastructure (real isolation between tenants).

filesystemAccess.note: Local server doesn't touch host fs; downloads happen on Browserbase side.

networkEgress.note: Agent navigates anywhere — that's the whole point. Lock down target URLs at the prompt level.

telemetry.note: Browserbase logs every session for billing + debugging. Their server-side retention policy applies.

dataResidency.note: Browserbase's infrastructure (US primarily). Page contents visited flow through their servers.

defaultPermissions.note: Cloud browser drives anywhere agent points it. No 'read-only browse' mode.

destructiveTools.note: Same shape as playwright-mcp — clicks and submits are destructive on remote systems. Gate at the host.

promptInjectionExposure.note: Same as any browser-driving MCP. Pages can contain hostile DOM. Treat all extracted text as untrusted.

supplyChainSurface.note: MCP SDK + Browserbase official client + Playwright (for some local fallbacks).

rateLimitRisk.note: Free tier: limited session minutes; paid tier: per-tier concurrency limits. Burst patterns hit rate limits faster than steady use.

verdictScope: Cloud browser automation where local Playwright has hit scaling, detection, or persistence walls.
verdictNote: Worth it when local hits a wall. For most projects, playwright-mcp local is sufficient and avoids the third-party data flow. Read Browserbase's privacy and retention docs before pointing agents at PII-handling sites.
